hook.text: Your JavaScript tries to call another domain's API. The browser blocks it. CORS is why — and the fix.

buildup.text: Same-origin policy blocks cross-origin requests by default. It prevents a malicious page from stealing your data.

discovery.text: CORS lets servers opt in. The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header tells browsers which origins are allowed.

twist.text: Preflight OPTIONS requests check permission before the real request. The browser sends them automatically.

climax.text: Misconfigured CORS — like allowing all origins — undoes the entire security model. Be specific.

punchline.text: CORS controls cross-origin access. The server decides who's allowed.

quiz.question: What HTTP header does a server use to allow cross-origin requests?
